title: flaky festive thai scamorza pastry
description: a thai-inspired pastry featuring scamorza cheese and lamination puff, perfect for festivals and celebrations.
instructions: combine flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a mixing bowl.
add cold unsalted butter and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
add ice-cold water and mix until the dough comes together in a ball.
knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
mix together vegan butter, pandan extract, and activated charcoal powder in a separate bowl.
spread the vegan butter mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
fold the dough in half lengthwise, then fold it in half again, rotating it 90 degrees after each fold.
roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch again.
cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
place a spoonful of coconut milk in the center of each dough circle.
top with a slice of scamorza cheese.
fold the dough over the cheese to form a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal.
brush the tops with coconut oil and sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds.
heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and cook the pastries for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
brush with sesame oil and serve warm.
serve with a sprinkle of palm sugar and a side of spicy Thai sauce.
serve with a side of steamed jasmine rice and a dollop of coconut cream.
serve with a side of spicy Thai chutney and a sprinkle of toasted peanuts.
serve with a side of cucumber raita and a sprinkle of toasted cumin seeds.
serve with a side of mixed greens salad and a drizzle of Thai dressing.
